runesv3.summonerSpellExplanation: Take <ScSummonerInline summonerIdentifier="Exhaust" /> instead vs burst like <ScChampionInline championId="Pantheon" /> <ScChampionInline championId="Riven" />

AdETOniAtA.insight:
<ScChampionInline championId="Darius" /> is ==insanely strong at all phases of the game==. These runes make sure you stay that way.

* <ScRuneInline runeIdentifier="Legend: Alacrity" /> is crucial for stacking passive faster. In late game teamfights, the extra attack speed lets you apply passive to ==new targets ASAP== after <ScAbilityInline_v2 spellKey="R" /> resets.
* Since your earlygame is already among the strongest in the game, you can take <ScRuneInline runeIdentifier="Gathering Storm" /> and ==scale for free==. Unlike other champions that need extra earlygame power from runes, <ScChampionInline championId="Darius" /> simply wins.

KqOfjaWw2C.insight:
<ScRuneInline runeIdentifier="Triumph" /> pairs perfectly with <ScChampionInline championId="Darius" />'s reset playstyle in teamfights. Since you're going to be building <ScItemInline itemName="Youmuu's Ghostblade" /> with this rune page, you will be ==squishier== than a "standard" <ScRuneInline runeIdentifier="Conqueror" /> <ScChampionInline championId="Darius" /> that only builds items with HP in it.

* The ==missing HP refund== solves this issue by increasing your effective HP bar, combined with your <ScAbilityInline_v2 spellKey="Q" /> that also heals based on missing HP.
* You're probably going to get a few kills in lane with this rune page. The economy acceleration from <ScRuneInline runeIdentifier="Triumph" /> can help you reach spikes quicker, and <ScItemInline itemName="Youmuu's Ghostblade" /> itself is a cheap item at ==2800 gold==.

itemGroups.insight: Rush <ScItemInline itemName="Youmuu's Ghostblade" />, prioritizing <ScItemInline itemName="Serrated Dirk" /> for max damage.Your boots can be any between <ScItemInline itemName="Plated Steelcaps" />, <ScItemInline itemName="Mercury's Treads" />, <ScItemInline itemName="Boots of Swiftness" />. Go <ScItemInline itemName="Dead Man's Plate" /> for mobility / tankiness. Alternatively, you can go <ScItemInline itemName="Force of Nature" /> if vs AP.
itemGroups.title: Core Build
Fourth Item Options
itemGroups.insight: If you didn't buy <ScItemInline itemName="Dead Man's Plate" />, you're skipping it this game. If you do have <ScItemInline itemName="Dead Man's Plate" />, buy a <ScItemInline itemName="Negatron Cloak" /> first for MR. Whether you have <ScItemInline itemName="Dead Man's Plate" /> or <ScItemInline itemName="Force of Nature" />, you're always going to buy <ScItemInline itemName="Death's Dance" /> here.

itemGroups.title: Fifth Item Options
itemGroups.insight: If you bought a <ScItemInline itemName="Negatron Cloak" />, finish into <ScItemInline itemName="Force of Nature" /> . If you already have <ScItemInline itemName="Force of Nature" />, you should instead buy <ScItemInline itemName="Sterak's Gage" />

Default to <ScItemInline itemName="Guardian Angel" />.<ScItemInline itemName="Mercurial Scimitar" /> is an amazing pick-up vs champs locking you down like <ScChampionInline championId="TwistedFate" /> <ScChampionInline championId="Lissandra" /><ScChampionInline championId="Malzahar" /> Buy <ScItemInline itemName="Randuin's Omen" /> if they have 2 crit champions like <ScChampionInline championId="Yone" />/<ScChampionInline championId="Caitlyn" />.
itemGroups.title: Sixth Item Options

content.content: If the enemy team has a or a comp who is extremely CC heavy, then consider buying a in the mid game after you've got your or and completed. If you can't move in a fight then it doesn't matter what kind of damage you deal or what resists you have, so value can be really underrated on in the correct spots.\n\nDon't complete your into until your last item, as working towards something like is much more valuable than completing .

When you pivot your build based on the 3rd item you bought (<ScItemInline itemName="Dead Man's Plate" /> vs <ScItemInline itemName="Force of Nature" />), you run into a scenario where you'll build an item you normally don't buy, and that is <ScItemInline itemName="Sterak's Gage" />. In the case that you bought <ScItemInline itemName="Force of Nature" /> in your core early, you'll be skipping <ScItemInline itemName="Dead Man's Plate" /> entirely.

* This is because you already have tons of mobility in your runes + items, and <ScItemInline itemName="Dead Man's Plate" />'s stats are underwhelming if you're building it 4th/5th compared to 3rd.
* Since you'll be building <ScItemInline itemName="Death's Dance" /> every game, you already have a source of armor - <ScItemInline itemName="Dead Man's Plate" /> wouldn't provide much value because of this.
* <ScItemInline itemName="Sterak's Gage" /> makes you deal more damage while also still being super tanky. It even provides 20% tenacity making you harder to kite.
